rulings.Case Title: WING WO MA VS. JUN TAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 3, 2012, Line 11. CROSS COMPLAINANTS KEVIN CURRAN AND CURRAN &. ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment is denied and alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the fourth, sixth, seventh and eighth causes of action, denied as to the third, fifth, ninth, tenth and eleventh causes of action, and taken off calendar as to the first and second causes of action. Because Ho and Tan are judicially estopped from asserting that they had a contract with Curran or his company, the fourth, sixth, seventh and eighth causes of action are without merit as a matter of law. The moving parties did not satisfy their burden of production as to the third, fifth, ninth, tenth and eleventh causes of action by either presenting evidence negating an element of those claims or showing that Ho and Tan do not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to prove those claims. Because the first and second causes of action are not alleged against the moving parties, there is no basis to address those claims on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC, VS. KILLARNEY CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motin And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Requests For Admissions (Set One) To Michael Murray And Mid-Market Development Co. In.C And For Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Tuesday, April 3, 2012, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT CONSTRUCTION TESTING SERVICES, INC.'s Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Requests For Admissions (Set One) To Michael Murray And Mid-Market Development Co. In.C And For Monetary Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions in amount of $670, to be split evenly by defendants and cross-defendants Michael Murray and Mid-Market Development Co. Inc. Requested sanctions of $1,090 are reduced to $670 because the $1,090 figure included $420 in estimated costs for reply briefing and attendance at hearing, neither of which has been or will be incurred by defendant and cross-complainenat Construction Testing Services, Inc. The Motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers, and: (1) the Motion is not opposed. (2) Michael Murray and Mid-Market Development Co. Inc. have not submitted any response or proposed response to the Requests for Admission (Set One). See Cal. Civ. Proc. Code section 2033.280(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com referencing the case name and number in the subject line with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN A. BURMEISTER, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE VS. 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 3, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC AND DIANA SACA'S Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike. Granted without leave to amend as to the prayer to invalidate the easement and denied as to all other allegations sought to be stricken. Plaintiff does not plead any legal basis to invalidate the easement nor does it appear that he can cure this deficiency. None of the claims are necessarily time-barred due to the application of the discovery rule. As to the certificate of compliance, Plaintiff alleges that 4055 LLC and Ms. Saca conspired with Ms. Sabella to commit tortuous conduct against plaintiff and did so for their own financial gain and thus plaintiff has sufficiently alleged the tort claims against 4055 LLC and Ms. Saca. Moreover, the concealment and constructive fraud claims can be pleaded directly against defendants based on their alleged concealment of the certificate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN A. BURMEISTER, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE VS. 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 3, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANTS 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC AND DIANA SACA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. The discovery rule applies and it cannot be determined as a matter of law that plaintiff had notice of the Exclusive Easement on March 22, 2007. Plaintiff alleges that he did not receive a complete copy of the Exclusive Easement and defendants misrepresented its scope. Plaintiff alleges that 4055 LLC and Ms. Saca conspired with Ms. Sabella to commit tortuous conduct against plaintiff and did so for their own financial gain and thus plaintiff has sufficiently alleged the tort claims against 4055 LLC and Ms. Saca. Moreover, the concealment and constructive fraud claims can be pleaded directly against defendants based on their alleged concealment of material facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
